g.text: File Filtering API - Class Diagram (Annotated for Interview)
Trace: "Find all .java files > 1MB modified in last 7 days, sorted by size." Follow Step 1 to 7.
hides how files are fetched (local disk,
S3, database) and applies the filter."
"A File has name, extension, size, path,
STEP 3 - Where do files come from?
Returns true if file passes the filter.
The system is built around this."
Not are themselves Filters — they implement
"build() wraps all criteria in an AndFilter.
"The entire system is built around one interface:
Filter.apply(file). Simple filters check one attribute (
Composite filters combine them with AND/OR/NOT (
Builder provides a fluent API. FileSource is pluggable.
The query 'all .java files > 1MB modified last week' becomes:
File model: name, size, extension, date, permissions
Files fetched from local disk / S3 / in-memory
Filter interface: one method apply(file) -> boolean
Leaf filters: Size, Extension, Name, Date, Permission
AndFilter, OrFilter, NotFilter combine any filters
FilterBuilder gives fluent API to construct filters
